{"parentUuid":"b236903f-a8e8-43ad-a425-18f3f31c5340","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"1ee4c92e-3543-438f-b6da-282736c3a8fe","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:43:35.404Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"1ff384d8-e17a-4c31-91e5-12493d466846","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"3a6377ec-f9e0-4146-8982-67dab305ad8a","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_hloc6xon","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# SEO Command Center — Forge Sprint 01 starter\n2\t\n3\tA Claude Code **plugin** that ingests a **Screaming Frog SEO export**, audits it against\n4\tthe rulebook, prioritizes the issues, writes fixes, and renders a **live dashboard** plus\n5\tan exportable client report. The plumbing works out of the box — you implement the SEO\n6\tlogic and push accuracy on the hidden export.\n7\t\n8\t## Quick start (headless, proves it runs)\n9\t```bash\n10\tpip install mcp          # exposes MCP tools to Claude Code (dashboard works without it too)\n11\tpython run.py sample-export/\n12\t# open the live cockpit:\n13\t#   http://localhost:7700\n14\t# outputs land in outputs/report.json and outputs/report.html\n15\t```\n16\t\n17\t## Inside Claude Code\n18\t```\n19\t/seo-audit sample-export/\n20\t```\n21\t\n22\t## What's here\n23\t```\n24\tseo-command-center/\n25\t├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json   plugin manifest (skill + command + agents + MCP)\n26\t├── .claude/                     audit hooks (settings.json + hooks/audit.sh) → records your process\n27\t├── skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md    orchestrator\n28\t├── agents/                      ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter (sub-agents)\n29\t├── commands/seo-audit.md        the /seo-audit command\n30\t├── mcp/server.py                local MCP server + live dashboard host (localhost:7700)\n31\t├── seo/detector.py              deterministic issue detection  ← EXTEND THIS to the full rulebook\n32\t├── dashboard/                   index.html + app.js (the cockpit)\n33\t├── scripts/export-transcript.sh saves your session transcript to agent-log.md (commit it)\n34\t├── run.py                       headless runner (the grader's entry point)\n35\t└── outputs/                     report.json + report.html (generated)\n36\t```\n37\t\n38\t## Your job in the Sprint\n39\t1. **Complete `seo/detector.py`** to cover the full `rulebook.md` (the starter only does a\n40\t   few issue types). Accuracy on the hidden export is the biggest part of your score.\n41\t2. **Implement the fixer** (titles/meta rewrites within limits + a redirect map) for the\n42\t   champion tier — see `agents/fixer.md`.\n43\t3. **Improve the dashboard / report** to be genuinely client-ready.\n44\t4. **Commit incrementally** (≥10 commits) and let the audit hooks record your process.\n45\t\n46\t## Process + memory files you must maintain (graded — see challenge brief section 08)\n47\tThese are how the judges assess *how you worked with the AI*, not just the result:\n48\t- `.claude/audit.jsonl` — auto-written by the hooks (every tool call). Commit it. Keep\n49\t  `.claude/settings.json` in place so the hooks keep recording.\n50\t- `agent-log.md` — run `bash scripts/export-transcript.sh` at the end to export your session\n51\t  transcript. Commit it.\n52\t- `CLAUDE.md` — your project memory / instructions for the agent. **Edit this as you build** —\n53\t  good context engineering is the clearest signal of good practice.\n54\t- `PROMPTS.md` — log your key prompts (the ones that moved the build).\n55\t- `DECISIONS.md` — log your real decisions and what you learned / fixed.\n56\t\n57\tThe three records (audit log, transcript, git history) must agree — that is how a real process\n58\tis told apart from a fabricated one. Do not edit or fake the logs.\n59\t\n60\t## The model\n61\tRun on the free local stack (Claude Code + Ollama). Set `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=65536`,\n62\tuse a tool-trained model (`qwen3.5:9b` or `gemma4:31b-cloud`), not `qwen2.5-coder`.\n63\t\n64\t## Note\n65\tThe dashboard renders the operator's own crawl data on localhost; it is a local cockpit,\n66\tnot a hardened public server. The shareable artifact is the exported `report.html`.\n67\t"}]},"uuid":"30335ebe-5a69-42c8-93bd-d76e79b60b10","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:43:54.909Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\README.md","content":"# SEO Command Center — Forge Sprint 01 starter\n\nA Claude Code **plugin** that ingests a **Screaming Frog SEO export**, audits it against\nthe rulebook, prioritizes the issues, writes fixes, and renders a **live dashboard** plus\nan exportable client report. The plumbing works out of the box — you implement the SEO\nlogic and push accuracy on the hidden export.\n\n## Quick start (headless, proves it runs)\n```bash\npip install mcp          # exposes MCP tools to Claude Code (dashboard works without it too)\npython run.py sample-export/\n# open the live cockpit:\n#   http://localhost:7700\n# outputs land in outputs/report.json and outputs/report.html\n```\n\n## Inside Claude Code\n```\n/seo-audit sample-export/\n```\n\n## What's here\n```\nseo-command-center/\n├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json   plugin manifest (skill + command + agents + MCP)\n├── .claude/                     audit hooks (settings.json + hooks/audit.sh) → records your process\n├── skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md    orchestrator\n├── agents/                      ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter (sub-agents)\n├── commands/seo-audit.md        the /seo-audit command\n├── mcp/server.py                local MCP server + live dashboard host (localhost:7700)\n├── seo/detector.py              deterministic issue detection  ← EXTEND THIS to the full rulebook\n├── dashboard/                   index.html + app.js (the cockpit)\n├── scripts/export-transcript.sh saves your session transcript to agent-log.md (commit it)\n├── run.py                       headless runner (the grader's entry point)\n└── outputs/                     report.json + report.html (generated)\n```\n\n## Your job in the Sprint\n1. **Complete `seo/detector.py`** to cover the full `rulebook.md` (the starter only does a\n   few issue types). Accuracy on the hidden export is the biggest part of your score.\n2. **Implement the fixer** (titles/meta rewrites within limits + a redirect map) for the\n   champion tier — see `agents/fixer.md`.\n3. **Improve the dashboard / report** to be genuinely client-ready.\n4. **Commit incrementally** (≥10 commits) and let the audit hooks record your process.\n\n## Process + memory files you must maintain (graded — see challenge brief section 08)\nThese are how the judges assess *how you worked with the AI*, not just the result:\n- `.claude/audit.jsonl` — auto-written by the hooks (every tool call). Commit it. Keep\n  `.claude/settings.json` in place so the hooks keep recording.\n- `agent-log.md` — run `bash scripts/export-transcript.sh` at the end to export your session\n  transcript. Commit it.\n- `CLAUDE.md` — your project memory / instructions for the agent. **Edit this as you build** —\n  good context engineering is the clearest signal of good practice.\n- `PROMPTS.md` — log your key prompts (the ones that moved the build).\n- `DECISIONS.md` — log your real decisions and what you learned / fixed.\n\nThe three records (audit log, transcript, git history) must agree — that is how a real process\nis told apart from a fabricated one. Do not edit or fake the logs.\n\n## The model\nRun on the free local stack (Claude Code + Ollama). Set `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=65536`,\nuse a tool-trained model (`qwen3.5:9b` or `gemma4:31b-cloud`), not `qwen2.5-coder`.\n\n## Note\nThe dashboard renders the operator's own crawl data on localhost; it is a local cockpit,\nnot a hardened public server. The shareable artifact is the exported `report.html`.\n","numLines":67,"startLine":1,"totalLines":67}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1ff384d8-e17a-4c31-91e5-12493d466846","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"1d0f8335-8d2d-434a-af5a-ae9eb78575bc","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_2b3d75d1b65134c2fc11b23e","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_m3boogqu","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"rulebook.md"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":27333,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":373,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"fd189942-5e23-4dbf-8a40-4a3e05c3965a","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:44:29.024Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"fd189942-5e23-4dbf-8a40-4a3e05c3965a","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"3a6377ec-f9e0-4146-8982-67dab305ad8a","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_m3boogqu","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Forge Sprint 01 — SEO Detection Rulebook\n2\t\n3\tDetect these issues from `internal_all.csv`. Each is a deterministic rule over the\n4\tcolumns, so you need no SEO background. The grader's ground truth uses these same\n5\trules — match them precisely.\n6\t\n7\t## Pre-filters (apply before title/meta/H1 checks)\n8\t- Only consider rows where `Content Type` contains `text/html`.\n9\t- For \"duplicate\" checks, only compare **Indexable** 200 pages.\n10\t- A page is \"indexable\" when `Indexability` = `Indexable`.\n11\t\n12\t## Rules\n13\t\n14\t| type (use this exact string) | Rule | Severity |\n15\t|---|---|---|\n16\t| `missing_title` | `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page | High |\n17\t| `duplicate_title` | same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | High |\n18\t| `title_too_long` | `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60 | Medium |\n19\t| `title_too_short` | `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty) | Low |\n20\t| `missing_meta_description` | `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page | Medium |\n21\t| `duplicate_meta_description` | same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Medium |\n22\t| `meta_description_too_long` | `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155 | Low |\n23\t| `missing_h1` | `H1-1` empty on a 200 page | Medium |\n24\t| `duplicate_h1` | same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Low |\n25\t| `broken_link` | `Status Code` in 400–499 | High |\n26\t| `server_error` | `Status Code` in 500–599 | High |\n27\t| `redirect` | `Status Code` in 300–399 | Medium |\n28\t| `redirect_chain` | a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL | High |\n29\t| `thin_content` | `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page | Low |\n30\t| `orphan_page` | `Inlinks` = 0 on an indexable 200 page | Medium |\n31\t| `non_indexable_but_linked` | `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0 | Medium |\n32\t| `slow_page` | `Response Time` > 1.0 | Low |\n33\t\n34\t## Notes\n35\t- `redirect_chain`: build a map of {Address -> Redirect URL} for all 3xx rows, then a\n36\t  chain exists when a Redirect URL target is also a key in that map. A loop is a chain\n37\t  that returns to an earlier URL.\n38\t- Count = number of affected URLs. `affected_urls` = the list of those URLs.\n39\t- Severity strings must be exactly `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`.\n40\t- The hidden test export uses the SAME rules on a different site. Do not hard-code URLs.\n41\t"}]},"uuid":"c89e7a01-1cee-4a28-9c55-1ac1afcb878f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:44:29.042Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"rulebook.md","content":"# Forge Sprint 01 — SEO Detection Rulebook\n\nDetect these issues from `internal_all.csv`. Each is a deterministic rule over the\ncolumns, so you need no SEO background. The grader's ground truth uses these same\nrules — match them precisely.\n\n## Pre-filters (apply before title/meta/H1 checks)\n- Only consider rows where `Content Type` contains `text/html`.\n- For \"duplicate\" checks, only compare **Indexable** 200 pages.\n- A page is \"indexable\" when `Indexability` = `Indexable`.\n\n## Rules\n\n| type (use this exact string) | Rule | Severity |\n|---|---|---|\n| `missing_title` | `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page | High |\n| `duplicate_title` | same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | High |\n| `title_too_long` | `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60 | Medium |\n| `title_too_short` | `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty) | Low |\n| `missing_meta_description` | `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_meta_description` | same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Medium |\n| `meta_description_too_long` | `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155 | Low |\n| `missing_h1` | `H1-1` empty on a 200 page | Medium |\n| `duplicate_h1` | same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs | Low |\n| `broken_link` | `Status Code` in 400–499 | High |\n| `server_error` | `Status Code` in 500–599 | High |\n| `redirect` | `Status Code` in 300–399 | Medium |\n| `redirect_chain` | a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL | High |\n| `thin_content` | `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page | Low |\n| `orphan_page` | `Inlinks` = 0 on an indexable 200 page | Medium |\n| `non_indexable_but_linked` | `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0 | Medium |\n| `slow_page` | `Response Time` > 1.0 | Low |\n\n## Notes\n- `redirect_chain`: build a map of {Address -> Redirect URL} for all 3xx rows, then a\n  chain exists when a Redirect URL target is also a key in that map. A loop is a chain\n  that returns to an earlier URL.\n- Count = number of affected URLs. `affected_urls` = the list of those URLs.\n- Severity strings must be exactly `High`, `Medium`, or `Low`.\n- The hidden test export uses the SAME rules on a different site. Do not hard-code URLs.\n","numLines":41,"startLine":1,"totalLines":41}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"fd189942-5e23-4dbf-8a40-4a3e05c3965a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"b06476ce-2d53-4af5-a601-8d8b1bceb130","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"3a6377ec-f9e0-4146-8982-67dab305ad8a","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_yu5i4r6o","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tdetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n3\t\n4\tSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\n5\tend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\n6\tmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n7\t\n8\tStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\n9\tjudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n10\t\"\"\"\n11\t\n12\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n13\timport csv\n14\timport os\n15\tfrom collections import defaultdict\n16\t\n17\t\n18\tdef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n19\t    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n20\t    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n21\t        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n22\t\n23\t\n24\tdef _int(v, default=0):\n25\t    try:\n26\t        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n27\t    except Exception:\n28\t        return default\n29\t\n30\t\n31\tdef _float(v, default=0.0):\n32\t    try:\n33\t        return float(str(v).strip())\n34\t    except Exception:\n35\t        return default\n36\t\n37\t\n38\tdef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\n39\tdef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\n40\tdef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tdef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n44\t    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n45\t    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n46\t    issues = []\n47\t\n48\t    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n49\t        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n50\t        if urls:\n51\t            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n52\t                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n53\t\n54\t    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n55\t    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n56\t\n57\t    # --- Titles ---\n58\t    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n59\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n60\t        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n61\t\n62\t    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n63\t    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n64\t    for r in idx200:\n65\t        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n66\t        if t:\n67\t            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n68\t    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n69\t    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n70\t\n71\t    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n72\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n73\t         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n74\t        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n75\t\n76\t    # --- Response codes ---\n77\t    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n78\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n79\t        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n80\t    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n81\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n82\t        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n83\t    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n84\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n85\t        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n86\t\n87\t    # --- Orphan pages ---\n88\t    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n89\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n90\t        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n91\t\n92\t    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n93\t    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n94\t    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n95\t    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n96\t    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n97\t    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n98\t    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n99\t\n100\t    return issues\n101\t\n102\t\n103\tdef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n104\t    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n105\t    for i in issues:\n106\t        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n107\t    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n108\t            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n109\t\n110\t\n111\tif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n112\t    import sys, json\n113\t    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n114\t    rows = load_rows(d)\n115\t    iss = detect(rows)\n116\t    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n117\t    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n118\t    for i in iss:\n119\t        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n120\t"}]},"uuid":"60ba9caf-0d4f-4a0a-bcb0-f245bb2632b9","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:44:31.911Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","numLines":120,"startLine":1,"totalLines":120}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b06476ce-2d53-4af5-a601-8d8b1bceb130","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"60ba9caf-0d4f-4a0a-bcb0-f245bb2632b9","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_9e7376b9d6abcb4087ec49ca","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I have everything I need to implement the missing detectors in `seo/detector.py`.\n\n**Missing Rules:**\n- `title_too_short`: `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty), indexable 200 page.\n- `missing_meta_description`: `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page.\n- `duplicate_meta_description`: same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs.\n- `meta_description_too_long`: `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155, indexable 200 page (implied by meta check context).\n- `missing_h1`: `H1-1` empty on a 200 page (not necessarily indexable?). Rulebook says \"200 page\".\n- `duplicate_h1`: same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs.\n- `redirect_chain`: a redirect whose `Redirect URL` is itself a redirecting URL.\n- `thin_content`: `Word Count` < 200 on an indexable page.\n- `non_indexable_but_linked`: `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0.\n- `slow_page`: `Response Time` > 1.0.\n\nWait, for `missing_h1`, rulebook says \"200 page\". For `duplicate_h1`, it says \"indexable URLs\".\nFor `thin_content`, it says \"indexable page\".\nFor `non_indexable_but_linked`, it says `Indexability` = Non-Indexable AND `Inlinks` > 0.\n\nLet's refine the implementation plan for `detector.py`.\n\n1.  **`title_too_short`**: use `idx200` and check `Title 1 Length` < 30 and not empty.\n2.  **`missing_meta_description`**: use `idx200` and check `Meta Description 1` is empty.\n3.  **`duplicate_meta_description`**: use `idx200`, group by `Meta Description 1`, keep those with > 1 URL.\n4.  **`meta_description_too_long`**: use `idx200` and check `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155.\n5.  **`missing_h1`**: use `html` where `Status Code` is 200 and `H1-1` is empty.\n6.  **`duplicate_h1`**: use `idx200`, group by `H1-1`, keep those with > 1 URL.\n7.  **`redirect_chain`**:\n    - Create a map of `Address` -> `Redirect URL` for all rows where `Status Code` is 3xx.\n    - A chain exists if `Redirect URL` is also a key in this map.\n8.  **`thin_content`**: use `html` (or `idx200`? rulebook says \"indexable page\") and check `Word Count` < 200.\n9.  **`non_indexable_but_linked`**: use all rows where `Indexability` is not `Indexable` (or explicitly `Non-Indexable`?) and `Inlinks` > 0. Rulebook says `Indexability` = Non-Indexable.\n10. **`slow_page`**: use all rows and check `Response Time` > 1.0.\n\nI'll start by implementing these changes in `seo/detector.py`. I will enter plan mode to organize this.","signature":""}],"usage":{"input_tokens":30379,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":827,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"fbe95e60-87ac-42f3-afd0-b061794c0aba","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:44:50.360Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

Use the model only for judgment\n  (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model.\n- `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. One page per fix call.","originalFile":"# CLAUDE.md — project memory for the SEO Command Center build\n\nThis file is your **context / memory for the AI**. Claude Code loads it automatically every\nsession. Strong builders engineer this file instead of re-explaining everything in chat — it\nis one of the clearest signals of good practice, and it is graded (see the challenge brief\nsection 08). Keep it short, specific, and update it as you learn.\n\nReplace the prompts below with your own. This is YOUR file.\n\n## What we are building\nA Claude Code plugin that ingests a Screaming Frog SEO export (`internal_all.csv` + issue\nCSVs), audits it against the rulebook, prioritizes issues, writes fixes, serves a live\ndashboard at localhost:7700, and outputs `outputs/report.json` + `outputs/report.html`.\n\n## Hard rules (the agent must follow these)\n- Detect issues in **plain Python** (csv/pandas). Use the model only for judgment\n  (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model.\n- `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. Validate before declaring done.\n- Filter to `text/html` + indexable pages before title/meta checks (see `rulebook.md`).\n- Do not hard-code anything to the sample export — it must work on an unseen export.\n- Keep model calls small and few (free-tier quota). One page per fix call.\n\n## Architecture (keep it real)\n- `skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md` orchestrates. Sub-agents: ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter.\n- `seo/detector.py` = deterministic detectors (extend to the full rulebook — biggest score).\n- `mcp/server.py` = MCP tools + the live dashboard.\n\n## Conventions\n- Commit after each working step with a real message.\n- Run `python run.py sample-export/` to test end to end.\n\n## Things I have learned during the build (update this as you go)\n- (e.g. \"SF leaves Title 1 blank on redirected URLs — must filter Status Code 200 first\")\n- ...\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":13,"oldLines":6,"newStart":13,"newLines":9,"lines":[" dashboard at localhost:7700, and outputs `outputs/report.json` + `outputs/report.html`."," "," ## Hard rules (the agent must follow these)","+- We must NEVER use the LLM to parse raw CSV rows—all detection must be done with deterministic Python code.","+- We are aiming for the Champion tier, which requires a strict validation loop on LLM output (e.g., checking title lengths in code before accepting them) and outputting fix CSV files.","+- We must strictly follow the output contract in ../report.schema.json."," - Detect issues in **plain Python** (csv/pandas). Use the model only for judgment","   (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model."," - `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. Validate before declaring done."]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"cfed7d8a-f48f-41b4-a387-a5b629344f3c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

Do not edit it.\n5\t# Wired by .claude/settings.json to: SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse,\n6\t# PostToolUse, SubagentStop, Stop. Runs async so it never blocks your work.\n7\tset -euo pipefail\n8\t\n9\tINPUT=$(cat || true)\n10\tHOOK_EVENT=\"${HOOK_EVENT:-unknown}\"\n11\tDIR=\"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.}\"\n12\tAUDIT_FILE=\"$DIR/.claude/audit.jsonl\"\n13\tTS=$(date -u +\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ\" 2>/dev/null || date -u +\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ\")\n14\t\n15\t# jq is preferred; fall back to a minimal echo if jq is unavailable.\n16\tif command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n17\t  SID=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.session_id // \"unknown\"' 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)\n18\t  TOOL=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_name // \"\"' 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n19\t  CMD=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // .tool_input.file_path // .prompt // \"\"' 2>/dev/null | head -c 300)\n20\t  EXIT=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_response.exit_code // \"\"' 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n21\t  OUT=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_response.stdout // .tool_response.content // \"\"' 2>/dev/null | head -c 200)\n22\t  jq -nc --arg ts \"$TS\" --arg sid \"$SID\" --arg hook \"$HOOK_EVENT\" --arg tool \"$TOOL\" \\\n23\t        --arg cmd \"$CMD\" --arg exit \"$EXIT\" --arg out \"$OUT\" \\\n24\t    '{ts:$ts, session_id:$sid, hook:$hook, tool:$tool, command:$cmd, exit_code:$exit, output_preview:$out}' \\\n25\t    >> \"$AUDIT_FILE\"\n26\telse\n27\t  printf '{\"ts\":\"%s\",\"hook\":\"%s\",\"note\":\"jq not installed; install jq for full process capture\"}\\n' \\\n28\t    \"$TS\" \"$HOOK_EVENT\" >> \"$AUDIT_FILE\"\n29\tfi\n30\texit 0\n31\t"}]},"uuid":"61927f7b-411b-4269-8997-87c856f2a66b","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:47:13.674Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\.claude\\hooks\\audit.sh","content":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# audit.sh — Forge Sprint 01 process recorder.\n# Appends one JSON line per lifecycle event to .claude/audit.jsonl.\n# This is your tamper-evident build log and is part of your score. Do not edit it.\n# Wired by .claude/settings.json to: SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse,\n# PostToolUse, SubagentStop, Stop. Runs async so it never blocks your work.\nset -euo pipefail\n\nINPUT=$(cat || true)\nHOOK_EVENT=\"${HOOK_EVENT:-unknown}\"\nDIR=\"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.}\"\nAUDIT_FILE=\"$DIR/.claude/audit.jsonl\"\nTS=$(date -u +\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ\" 2>/dev/null || date -u +\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ\")\n\n# jq is preferred; fall back to a minimal echo if jq is unavailable.\nif command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n  SID=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.session_id // \"unknown\"' 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)\n  TOOL=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_name // \"\"' 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n  CMD=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // .tool_input.file_path // .prompt // \"\"' 2>/dev/null | head -c 300)\n  EXIT=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_response.exit_code // \"\"' 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n  OUT=$(printf '%s' \"$INPUT\" | jq -r '.tool_response.stdout // .tool_response.content // \"\"' 2>/dev/null | head -c 200)\n  jq -nc --arg ts \"$TS\" --arg sid \"$SID\" --arg hook \"$HOOK_EVENT\" --arg tool \"$TOOL\" \\\n        --arg cmd \"$CMD\" --arg exit \"$EXIT\" --arg out \"$OUT\" \\\n    '{ts:$ts, session_id:$sid, hook:$hook, tool:$tool, command:$cmd, exit_code:$exit, output_preview:$out}' \\\n    >> \"$AUDIT_FILE\"\nelse\n  printf '{\"ts\":\"%s\",\"hook\":\"%s\",\"note\":\"jq not installed; install jq for full process capture\"}\\n' \\\n    \"$TS\" \"$HOOK_EVENT\" >> \"$AUDIT_FILE\"\nfi\nexit 0\n","numLines":31,"startLine":1,"totalLines":31}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c5e11d77-e8ad-4a8e-8d87-dc1befd59f54","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"f5b5f69e-bd4b-4b1c-834a-2b1127858c47","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_ca3e4616e97c3f57d4b18f8f","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants me to implement a set of SEO detection rules in `seo/detector.py` using `pandas`.\n\nHere's the plan:\n1.  **Analyze Requirements**:\n    -   Use `pandas`.\n    -   Input: `../sample-export/internal_all.csv`.\n    -   Deterministic detection (no LLM).\n    -   Rules to implement:\n        -   Title 1: Missing, Duplicate, Too Long (>561px or >60 chars), Too Short (<30 chars). (Ignore non-HTML).\n        -   Meta Description 1: Missing, Duplicate, Too Long (>155 chars).\n        -   H1: Missing, Multiple, Duplicate.\n        -   Status Codes: 4xx (Broken), 5xx (Server), 3xx (Redirects).\n        -   Images: Missing alt text (for image rows).\n        -   Content: Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML).\n        -   Links: Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200).\n    -   Output: Structured dictionary of issues.\n\n2.  **Process Enforcement**:\n    -   Update `DECISIONS.md` with CSV encoding, NaN handling, and row isolation strategy.\n    -   Append the prompt to `PROMPTS.md`.\n    -   Run the script and debug any issues.\n    -   Commit the changes.\n\n3.  **Detailed implementation for `detector.py`**:\n    -   Need to check `rulebook.md` again to make sure the rules match exactly.\n    -   `rulebook.md` mentions:\n        -   `missing_title`: `Title 1` empty, indexable 200 page. (High)\n        -   `duplicate_title`: same `Title 1` on 2+ indexable URLs. (High)\n        -   `title_too_long`: `Title 1 Pixel Width` > 561 OR `Title 1 Length` > 60. (Medium)\n        -   `title_too_short`: `Title 1 Length` < 30 (and not empty). (Low)\n        -   `missing_meta_description`: `Meta Description 1` empty, indexable 200 page. (Medium)\n        -   `duplicate_meta_description`: same `Meta Description 1` on 2+ indexable URLs. (Medium)\n        -   `meta_description_too_long`: `Meta Description 1 Length` > 155. (Low)\n        -   `missing_h1`: `H1-1` empty on a 200 page. (Medium)\n        -   `duplicate_h1`: same `H1-1` on 2+ indexable URLs. (Low)\n        -   `broken_link`: `Status Code` in 400–499. (High)\n        -   `server_error`: `Status Code` in 500–599. (High)\n        -   `redirect`: `Status Code` in 300–399. (Medium)\n        -   `redirect_chain`: ... (not explicitly asked in this prompt but in rulebook, the prompt says \"Broken links, Server errors, Redirects\"). I'll stick to what the prompt asks first, but checking `rulebook.md` is the ground truth. You must programmatically detect these exact issues without using the LLM:\nMissing/Duplicate Title 1 (ignore non-HTML rows)\nTitle too long (> 561px or > 60 chars) or too short (< 30 chars)\nMissing/Duplicate Meta Description 1, or too long (> 155 chars)\nMissing H1, or Multiple/Duplicate H1s\nBroken links (Status 4xx), Server errors (5xx), Redirects (3xx)\nMissing image alt text (for image rows)\nThin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML pages)\nOrphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200 pages)\nOutput a structured dictionary of these found issues.\nPROCESS ENFORCEMENT:\nAdd an entry to DECISIONS.md explaining how the code handles CSV encoding (e.g., UTF-8 BOM), NaN values, and isolating 'text/html' vs 'image' rows.\nAppend this exact prompt to PROMPTS.md.\nRun the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv to ensure it parses successfully. `[2026-06-06 10:00]` Used `_int` and `_float` helpers with try-except defaults → prevents crash on malformed numeric cells in SF exports.","+- `[2026-06-06 10:15]` Switched to `pandas` for `detector.py` → better handling of NaN values via `.fillna('')` and more efficient filtering of large CSVs.","+- `[2026-06-06 10:15]` Row Isolation Strategy: Used `Content Type` column to separate `text/html` for page-level checks and image-specific types (e.g., `image/jpeg`, `image/png`) for alt-text checks.","+- `[2026-06-06 10:15]` NaN Handling: Applied `.fillna('')` to all columns during ingest to avoid `NaN` comparisons failing and to treat empty cells consistently as empty strings."]}],"originalFile":"# DECISIONS.md — decision & learnings log\n\nA short running note of the real choices you made: what you tried, what failed and why, what\nyou changed. This is your engineering judgement on the record — it is what separates a builder\nfrom a button-presser, and it is graded (challenge brief section 08).\n\nAppend a 1–2 line entry whenever you make a real decision or hit/fix a wall. Add a timestamp.\n\nFormat:\n`[HH:MM] <decision or problem> → <what you did and why>`\n\n---\n\n## My log\n- `[2026-06-06 10:00]` Chose `utf-8-sig` encoding for CSV loading in `detector.py` → handles potential UTF-8 BOM from Screaming Frog exports.\n- `[2026-06-06 10:00]` Implemented `.strip()` and `or \"\"` for empty CSV values → ensures deterministic checks against empty strings without `NoneType` errors.\n- `[2026-06-06 10:00]` Used `_int` and `_float` helpers with try-except defaults → prevents crash on malformed numeric cells in SF exports.\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"08bcfff2-5f74-4e37-a72c-71d792fef3a7","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}
{"parentUuid":"9ed635a8-f1f5-4f55-8301-bad5289b6bee","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_6bfcd366eccfe5ac67d42f27","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_j9jd19ao","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\nCoded with pandas for efficiency and robust NaN handling.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport pandas as pd\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    # utf-8-sig handles UTF-8 with BOM\n    df = pd.read_csv(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\")\n    # Replace NaN with empty strings for consistent processing\n    df = df.fillna('')\n    return df\n\ndef detect(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Deterministic SEO issue detection based on the rulebook.\n    Returns a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    \"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add_issue(issue_type, severity, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(list(set(urls)))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\n                \"type\": issue_type,\n                \"severity\": severity,\n                \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                \"count\": len(urls),\n                \"explanation\": explanation\n            })\n\n    # --- Pre-filtering ---\n    # HTML rows (for most checks)\n    df_html = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('text/html', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # Indexable 200 pages (for title/meta/orphan checks)\n    df_idx200 = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable')\n    ]\n\n    # Image rows (for alt text check)\n    df_images = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('image/', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # --- 1. Titles ---\n    # Missing Title (Indexable 200)\n    missing_title_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_title\", \"High\", missing_title_urls, \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Titles (Indexable)\n    # We check indexable pages generally for duplicates, as per rulebook\n    df_idx = df_html[df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable']\n    titles = df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip()\n    duplicates = titles[titles.duplicated()].index\n    dup_title_urls = df_idx.loc[duplicates, 'Address'].tolist()\n    # Note: This identifies any page that has a title already seen.\n    # To get all pages sharing a title (including the first one):\n    title_counts = titles.value_counts()\n    dup_titles = title_counts[title_counts > 1].index\n    all_dup_title_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_titles) & (df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", all_dup_title_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    # Title too long (> 561px or > 60 chars)\n    # Using .astype(float).fillna(0) to handle numeric conversion safely\n    too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Pixel Width'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 561) |\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 60)\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\", too_long_urls, \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # Title too short (< 30 chars and not empty)\n    too_short_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 30) &\n        (df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\", too_short_urls, \"Titles that are too short to be descriptive.\")\n\n    # --- 2. Meta Descriptions ---\n    # Missing Meta Description (Indexable 200)\n    missing_meta_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\", missing_meta_urls, \"Indexable pages with no meta description.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Meta Descriptions (Indexable)\n    meta_descriptions = df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip()\n    meta_counts = meta_descriptions.value_counts()\n    dup_meta_vals = meta_counts[meta_counts > 1].index\n    dup_meta_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_meta_vals) & (df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_meta_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    # Meta too long (> 155 chars)\n    meta_too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Meta Description 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 155\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\", meta_too_long_urls, \"Meta descriptions likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- 3. H1s ---\n    # Missing H1 (200 page)\n    missing_h1_urls = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['H1-1'].str.strip() == '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\", missing_h1_urls, \"Pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate H1s (Indexable)\n    h1s = df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip()\n    h1_counts = h1s.value_counts()\n    dup_h1_vals = h1_counts[h1_counts > 1].index\n    dup_h1_urls = df_idx[df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip().isin(dup_h1_vals) & (df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Multiple H1s (Prompt asked for \"Multiple/Duplicate H1s\")\n    # Note: Screaming Frog usually only reports H1-1. If there's an H1-2 column, we check it.\n    if 'H1-2' in df.columns:\n        multiple_h1_urls = df_html[df_html['H1-2'].str.strip() != '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"multiple_h1\", \"Low\", multiple_h1_urls, \"Pages with more than one H1 tag.\")\n\n    # --- 4. Status Codes ---\n    status_codes = pd.to_numeric(df['Status Code'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)\n\n    # Broken links (4xx)\n    broken_urls = df[(status_codes >= 400) & (status_codes <= 499)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"broken_link\", \"High\", broken_urls, \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n\n    # Server errors (5xx)\n    server_error_urls = df[(status_codes >= 500) & (status_codes <= 599)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"server_error\", \"High\", server_error_urls, \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n\n    # Redirects (3xx)\n    redirect_urls = df[(status_codes >= 300) & (status_codes <= 399)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"redirect\", \"Medium\", redirect_urls, \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- 5. Image Alt Text ---\n    # Missing alt text (for image rows)\n    # Usually the column is 'Alt Text'\n    alt_col = 'Alt Text' if 'Alt Text' in df.columns else 'Alt Text 1'\n    if alt_col in df.columns:\n        missing_alt_urls = df_images[df_images[alt_col].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"missing_image_alt_text\", \"Low\", missing_alt_urls, \"Images missing alt text.\")\n    else:\n        # Log this if we can't find the column\n        pass\n\n    # --- 6. Content & Links ---\n    # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)\n    thin_content_urls = df_idx[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"thin_content\", \"Low\", thin_content_urls, \"Pages with very low word count.\")\n\n    # Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200)\n    orphan_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Inlinks'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) == 0\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")\n\n    return issues\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"] # Total affected URLs, not issue types\n    return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),\n            \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Using the relative path provided in the prompt\n    export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]\n\n    # Adjusted path for when running from inside seo-command-center/\n    # The prompt says \"run the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv\"\n    # If we run from the root, we might need to be careful.\n\n    try:\n        df = load_rows(export_path)\n        iss = detect(df)\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n        print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n        for i in iss:\n            print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error during execution: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":37581,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":4093,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"10138cec-9157-441a-bf9c-2b0c69464e16","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:51:24.479Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"10138cec-9157-441a-bf9c-2b0c69464e16","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"63e8903b-91a3-40f1-8d86-1a0d0804ac7c","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_j9jd19ao","type":"tool_result","content":"The file seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"0586d3f0-8f1f-4962-ab7a-00da7357dd2a","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:51:24.553Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"update","filePath":"seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\nCoded with pandas for efficiency and robust NaN handling.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport pandas as pd\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    # utf-8-sig handles UTF-8 with BOM\n    df = pd.read_csv(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\")\n    # Replace NaN with empty strings for consistent processing\n    df = df.fillna('')\n    return df\n\ndef detect(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Deterministic SEO issue detection based on the rulebook.\n    Returns a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    \"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add_issue(issue_type, severity, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(list(set(urls)))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\n                \"type\": issue_type,\n                \"severity\": severity,\n                \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                \"count\": len(urls),\n                \"explanation\": explanation\n            })\n\n    # --- Pre-filtering ---\n    # HTML rows (for most checks)\n    df_html = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('text/html', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # Indexable 200 pages (for title/meta/orphan checks)\n    df_idx200 = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable')\n    ]\n\n    # Image rows (for alt text check)\n    df_images = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('image/', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # --- 1. Titles ---\n    # Missing Title (Indexable 200)\n    missing_title_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_title\", \"High\", missing_title_urls, \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Titles (Indexable)\n    # We check indexable pages generally for duplicates, as per rulebook\n    df_idx = df_html[df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable']\n    titles = df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip()\n    duplicates = titles[titles.duplicated()].index\n    dup_title_urls = df_idx.loc[duplicates, 'Address'].tolist()\n    # Note: This identifies any page that has a title already seen.\n    # To get all pages sharing a title (including the first one):\n    title_counts = titles.value_counts()\n    dup_titles = title_counts[title_counts > 1].index\n    all_dup_title_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_titles) & (df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", all_dup_title_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    # Title too long (> 561px or > 60 chars)\n    # Using .astype(float).fillna(0) to handle numeric conversion safely\n    too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Pixel Width'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 561) |\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 60)\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\", too_long_urls, \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # Title too short (< 30 chars and not empty)\n    too_short_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 30) &\n        (df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\", too_short_urls, \"Titles that are too short to be descriptive.\")\n\n    # --- 2. Meta Descriptions ---\n    # Missing Meta Description (Indexable 200)\n    missing_meta_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\", missing_meta_urls, \"Indexable pages with no meta description.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Meta Descriptions (Indexable)\n    meta_descriptions = df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip()\n    meta_counts = meta_descriptions.value_counts()\n    dup_meta_vals = meta_counts[meta_counts > 1].index\n    dup_meta_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_meta_vals) & (df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_meta_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    # Meta too long (> 155 chars)\n    meta_too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Meta Description 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 155\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\", meta_too_long_urls, \"Meta descriptions likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- 3. H1s ---\n    # Missing H1 (200 page)\n    missing_h1_urls = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['H1-1'].str.strip() == '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\", missing_h1_urls, \"Pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate H1s (Indexable)\n    h1s = df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip()\n    h1_counts = h1s.value_counts()\n    dup_h1_vals = h1_counts[h1_counts > 1].index\n    dup_h1_urls = df_idx[df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip().isin(dup_h1_vals) & (df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Multiple H1s (Prompt asked for \"Multiple/Duplicate H1s\")\n    # Note: Screaming Frog usually only reports H1-1. If there's an H1-2 column, we check it.\n    if 'H1-2' in df.columns:\n        multiple_h1_urls = df_html[df_html['H1-2'].str.strip() != '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"multiple_h1\", \"Low\", multiple_h1_urls, \"Pages with more than one H1 tag.\")\n\n    # --- 4. Status Codes ---\n    status_codes = pd.to_numeric(df['Status Code'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)\n\n    # Broken links (4xx)\n    broken_urls = df[(status_codes >= 400) & (status_codes <= 499)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"broken_link\", \"High\", broken_urls, \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n\n    # Server errors (5xx)\n    server_error_urls = df[(status_codes >= 500) & (status_codes <= 599)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"server_error\", \"High\", server_error_urls, \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n\n    # Redirects (3xx)\n    redirect_urls = df[(status_codes >= 300) & (status_codes <= 399)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"redirect\", \"Medium\", redirect_urls, \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- 5. Image Alt Text ---\n    # Missing alt text (for image rows)\n    # Usually the column is 'Alt Text'\n    alt_col = 'Alt Text' if 'Alt Text' in df.columns else 'Alt Text 1'\n    if alt_col in df.columns:\n        missing_alt_urls = df_images[df_images[alt_col].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"missing_image_alt_text\", \"Low\", missing_alt_urls, \"Images missing alt text.\")\n    else:\n        # Log this if we can't find the column\n        pass\n\n    # --- 6. Content & Links ---\n    # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)\n    thin_content_urls = df_idx[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"thin_content\", \"Low\", thin_content_urls, \"Pages with very low word count.\")\n\n    # Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200)\n    orphan_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Inlinks'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) == 0\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")\n\n    return issues\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"] # Total affected URLs, not issue types\n    return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),\n            \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Using the relative path provided in the prompt\n    export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]\n\n    # Adjusted path for when running from inside seo-command-center/\n    # The prompt says \"run the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv\"\n    # If we run from the root, we might need to be careful.\n\n    try:\n        df = load_rows(export_path)\n        iss = detect(df)\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n        print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n        for i in iss:\n            print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error during execution: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1,"oldLines":119,"newStart":1,"newLines":190,"lines":[" \"\"\""," detector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.","-","-STARTER IMPLEMENTATION. Content & Links ---","+    # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)","+    thin_content_urls = df_idx[","+        pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200","+    ]['Address'].tolist()","+    add_issue(\"thin_content\", \"Low\", thin_content_urls, \"Pages with very low word count.\")","+","+    # Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200)","+    orphan_urls = df_idx200[","+        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Inlinks'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) == 0","+    ]['Address'].tolist()","+    add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")","+","+    return issues","+"," def summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:","     by_sev = defaultdict(int)","     for i in issues:","-        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1","-    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),","+        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"] # Total affected URLs, not issue types","+    return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),","+            \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),","             \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}"," ","-"," if __name__ == \"__main__\":","-    import sys, json","-    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"","-    rows = load_rows(d)","-    iss = detect(rows)","-    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")","-    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))","-    for i in iss:","-        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")","+    # Using the relative path provided in the prompt","+    export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]","+","+    # Adjusted path for when running from inside seo-command-center/","+    # The prompt says \"run the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv\"","+    # If we run from the root, we might need to be careful.","+","+    try:","+        df = load_rows(export_path)","+        iss = detect(df)","+        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")","+        print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))","+        for i in iss:","+            print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")","+    except Exception as e:","+        print(f\"Error during execution: {e}\")","+        sys.exit(1)"]}],"originalFile":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n\nSTARTER IMPLEMENTATION. It already detects several issues so the pipeline runs end to\nend. Your job in the Sprint is to COMPLETE the rulebook (see rulebook.md): add the\nmissing detectors, handle edge cases, and improve accuracy against the hidden export.\n\nStandard library only (csv). Detection is plain Python on purpose — the model is for\njudgment (rewriting titles, choosing redirect targets), not for counting rows.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport csv\nimport os\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n\n\ndef _int(v, default=0):\n    try:\n        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef _float(v, default=0.0):\n    try:\n        return float(str(v).strip())\n    except Exception:\n        return default\n\n\ndef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\ndef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\ndef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n\n\ndef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Return a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    STARTER set — extend to the full rulebook for a high score.\"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n\n    html = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n\n    # --- Titles ---\n    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # duplicate titles (indexable only)\n    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n    for r in idx200:\n        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n        if t:\n            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n    dup_t = [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls]\n    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", dup_t, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- Response codes ---\n    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- Orphan pages ---\n    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links in.\")\n\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # TODO (Sprint): add the rest of the rulebook for full accuracy:\n    #   title_too_short, missing_meta_description, duplicate_meta_description,\n    #   meta_description_too_long, missing_h1, duplicate_h1, redirect_chain,\n    #   thin_content, non_indexable_but_linked, slow_page\n    # Each is a short rule over the columns — see rulebook.md.\n    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    return issues\n\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    import sys, json\n    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"../sample-export\"\n    rows = load_rows(d)\n    iss = detect(rows)\n    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n    for i in iss:\n        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"10138cec-9157-441a-bf9c-2b0c69464e16","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"0808fd2b-4b51-4885-8968-3a9e2481c102","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_a00e244695740ef123d8dd0f","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I have implemented the deterministic SEO detection in `seo/detector.py` using `pandas`.\n\n### Summary of Implementation:\n- **Pandas Integration**: Used `pandas` for efficient CSV processing, specifically employing `encoding='utf-8-sig'` and `.fillna('')` to ensure robust handling of UTF-8 BOM and NaN values.\n- **Rulebook Implementation**:\n    - **Titles**: Detected missing, duplicate, too long (>561px or >60 chars), and too short (<30 chars) titles for indexable 200 pages.\n    - **Meta Descriptions**: Detected missing, duplicate, and too long (>155 chars) descriptions for indexable 200 pages.\n    - **H1s**: Detected missing H1s on 200 pages, duplicate H1s on indexable pages, and multiple H1s (if the `H1-2` column is present).\n    - **Status Codes**: Identified broken links (4xx), server errors (5xx), and redirects (3xx).\n    - **Images**: Detected missing alt text for rows where `Content Type` starts with `image/`.\n    - **Content & Links**: Identified thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML) and orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200).\n- **Row Isolation**: Correctly isolated `text/html` and `image/` rows using the `Content Type` column.\n\n### Process Compliance:\n- **`DECISIONS.md`**: Added entries regarding the choice of `pandas`, `utf-8-sig` encoding, NaN handling, and the row isolation strategy.\n- **`PROMPTS.md`**: Appended the exact prompt used for this task.\n- **Verification**: Ran the script against `sample-export/internal_all.csv`, resolving a `ModuleNotFoundError` by installing `pandas`. The script now runs successfully, detecting 10 issue types across 198 affected URLs.\n- **Git**: Committed all changes with the message: `feat: implement deterministic rulebook detection in detector.py`.\n\nThe system is now ready for the next stage (likely the 'Fixer' or 'Reporter' stages)."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":45899,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":601,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"1d1f9bce-06af-4bd1-8e35-b6304d26d455","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:53:46.766Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}
