{"parentUuid":"239950de-9c28-40cd-b344-c70835b279eb","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"7a6866f7-661f-4b9e-ad90-cae7241de736","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6qfvi5z2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tserver.py — local MCP server + live dashboard host (one process, two faces).\n3\t\n4\t  1. MCP tools over stdio  -> Claude Code calls: seo_load, seo_detect, seo_report, seo_export\n5\t  2. HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n6\t\n7\tSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\n8\tthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n9\t\n10\tNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\n11\tstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n12\t\"\"\"\n13\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n14\timport json, os, queue, threading, time\n15\tfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n16\t\n17\tHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n18\tROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\n19\tDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\n20\tOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\n21\tPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\n22\tMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n23\t\n24\timport sys\n25\tsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\n26\tfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n27\t\n28\tRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n29\t_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n30\t_lock = threading.Lock()\n31\t\n32\t\n33\tdef _emit(event, data):\n34\t    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n35\t    with _lock:\n36\t        for q in list(_subs):\n37\t            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n38\t            except Exception: pass\n39\t\n40\t\n41\t# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\n42\tdef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n43\t    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n44\t    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n45\t                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n46\t    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n47\t    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n48\t\n49\t\n50\tdef _guess_site(rows):\n51\t    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n52\t    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n53\t    try:\n54\t        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n55\t        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n56\t    except Exception:\n57\t        return \"unknown\"\n58\t\n59\t\n60\tdef seo_detect() -> dict:\n61\t    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n62\t    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n63\t    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n64\t    for i in issues:\n65\t        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n66\t    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n67\t    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n68\t\n69\t\n70\tdef _report_obj() -> dict:\n71\t    return {\n72\t        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n73\t        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n74\t        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n75\t        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n76\t        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n77\t        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n78\t        \"run_meta\": {\"model\": MODEL, \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n79\t                     \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0)},\n80\t    }\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tdef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n84\t    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n85\t    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n86\t\n87\t\n88\tdef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n89\t    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n90\t    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n91\t\n92\t\n93\tdef seo_report() -> dict:\n94\t    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n95\t    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n96\t    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n97\t    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n98\t\n99\t\n100\tdef seo_export() -> dict:\n101\t    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n102\t    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n103\t    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n104\t    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n105\t\n106\t\n107\tdef _render_html(o) -> str:\n108\t    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n109\t    rows = \"\".join(\n110\t        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n111\t        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n112\t        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n113\t        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n114\t    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n115\t    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n116\t<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n117\t<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n118\t.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n119\t.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n120\t.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\n121\ttable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\n122\tth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n123\t.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n124\t.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\n125\tul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n126\t<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n127\t<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n128\t<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n129\t<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n130\t<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n131\t<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n132\t<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n133\t<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n134\t\n135\t\n136\t# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\n137\tclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n138\t    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n139\t    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n140\t        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n141\t        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n142\t        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n143\t    def do_GET(self):\n144\t        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n145\t            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n146\t            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n147\t        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n148\t            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n149\t            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n150\t        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n151\t            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n152\t        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n153\t            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n154\t            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n155\t            q = queue.Queue()\n156\t            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n157\t            try:\n158\t                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n159\t                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n160\t                while True:\n161\t                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n162\t                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n163\t                    self.wfile.flush()\n164\t            except Exception: pass\n165\t            finally:\n166\t                with _lock:\n167\t                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n168\t        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n169\t\n170\t\n171\tdef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n172\t    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n173\t    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n174\t    return httpd\n175\t\n176\t\n177\tdef _run_mcp():\n178\t    try:\n179\t        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n180\t    except Exception:\n181\t        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n182\t        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n183\t    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n184\t\n185\t    @mcp.tool()\n186\t    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n187\t        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n188\t        return seo_load(export_dir)\n189\t\n190\t    @mcp.tool()\n191\t    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n192\t        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n193\t        return seo_detect()\n194\t\n195\t    @mcp.tool()\n196\t    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n197\t        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n198\t        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n199\t\n200\t    @mcp.tool()\n201\t    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n202\t        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n203\t        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n204\t\n205\t    @mcp.tool()\n206\t    def write_report() -> dict:\n207\t        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n208\t        return seo_report()\n209\t\n210\t    @mcp.tool()\n211\t    def export_report() -> dict:\n212\t        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n213\t        return seo_export()\n214\t\n215\t    mcp.run()\n216\t\n217\t\n218\tif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n219\t    start_dashboard()\n220\t    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n221\t    _run_mcp()\n222\t"}]},"uuid":"d58637b8-16a2-478d-807b-9c139852740a","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:55:00.646Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py","content":"\"\"\"\nserver.py — local MCP server + live dashboard host (one process, two faces).\n\n  1. MCP tools over stdio  -> Claude Code calls: seo_load, seo_detect, seo_report, seo_export\n  2. HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n\nSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\nthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n\nNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\nstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport json, os, queue, threading, time\nfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\nDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\nPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\nMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\nfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n\nRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n\ndef _emit(event, data):\n    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n    with _lock:\n        for q in list(_subs):\n            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n            except Exception: pass\n\n\n# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\ndef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n\n\ndef _guess_site(rows):\n    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n    try:\n        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n    except Exception:\n        return \"unknown\"\n\n\ndef seo_detect() -> dict:\n    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n    for i in issues:\n        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n\n\ndef _report_obj() -> dict:\n    return {\n        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n        \"run_meta\": {\"model\": MODEL, \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n                     \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0)},\n    }\n\n\ndef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n\n\ndef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n\n\ndef seo_report() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef seo_export() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef _render_html(o) -> str:\n    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    rows = \"\".join(\n        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\ntable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\nth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\nul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n\n\n# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\nclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n    def do_GET(self):\n        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n            q = queue.Queue()\n            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n            try:\n                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n                while True:\n                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n                    self.wfile.flush()\n            except Exception: pass\n            finally:\n                with _lock:\n                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n\n\ndef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n    return httpd\n\n\ndef _run_mcp():\n    try:\n        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n    except Exception:\n        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n        return seo_load(export_dir)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n        return seo_detect()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def write_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n        return seo_report()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def export_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n        return seo_export()\n\n    mcp.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    start_dashboard()\n    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n    _run_mcp()\n","numLines":222,"startLine":1,"totalLines":222}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"239950de-9c28-40cd-b344-c70835b279eb","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

When `seo_detect()` is called in `server.py`, it calls `detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))`.\n\nHowever, `detector.detect` is a single function that returns the list of issues at the end. To provide \"live\" updates (e.g., `urls_processed`), `detector.detect` needs to be able to notify the server *while* it's processing.\n\nThe user suggested:\n- \"Modify mcp/server.py to expose a state-management system... that can receive live updates from detector.py\"\n- \"Update detector.py to send brief status updates to this server via HTTP POST or direct import depending on the architecture.\"\n\nIf `detector.py` is imported by `server.py` and executed in the same process, it can just call a function in `server.py`. But `detector.py` is also designed to be run as a script.\n\nTo support both, a simple HTTP endpoint in `H` (the request handler in `server.py`) would be best.\n\n**Plan**:\n1.  **Modify `mcp/server.py`**:\n    *   Add a `do_POST` method to the `H` class to handle updates to the `RUN` state.\n    *   The POST endpoint `/update` will accept JSON like `{\"current_stage\": \"...\", \"urls_processed\": 100, ...}`.\n    *   Update the `RUN` dictionary and call `_emit(\"update\", data)` to notify any connected dashboard clients via SSE.\n\n2.  **Modify `seo/detector.py`**:\n    *   Add a helper function `send_update(data)` that sends an HTTP POST request to `http://localhost:7700/update`.\n    *   In `detect()`, insert calls to `send_update` during the process (e.g., after filtering rows, after each major rule check).\n\n3.  **Log in `DECISIONS.md`**:\n    *   Explain the use of an HTTP POST endpoint for state updates to allow both standalone and integrated execution of the detector.\n\n4.  **Update `PROMPTS.md`**:\n    *   Append the prompt.\n\n5.  **Verification**:\n    *   Run `python seo-command-center/mcp/server.py`.\n\n6.  **Commit**:\n    *   `git add .`\n    *   `git commit -m \"feat: implement backend state management for MCP dashboard\"`\n\nLet's implement.\n\n**`mcp/server.py` changes**:\n- Add `do_POST` to `H`.\n- If `self.path == \"/update\"`, parse JSON and update `RUN`.\n\n**`seo/detector.py` changes**:\n- Import `requests` or use `urllib.request`. `urllib.request` is better as it's standard library.\n- Add `send_update` function.\n- Update `detect` to call `send_update`.\n\nWait, I should check if I can use `requests`. The project seems to prefer standard library if possible, but `pandas` was installed. `urllib.request` is safer for a \"standard library\" feel, but `requests` is easier. I'll use `urllib.request` to minimize dependencies unless `requests` is already there (which I don't know). Currently, it's just a few list comprehensions. This shows how you direct an AI, which is graded (challenge brief section 08).\n\nFormat per entry:\n- **Prompt** (paste it)\n- **For:** what you were trying to do\n- **Revised?** did you have to change it, and why\n\n---\n\n## My prompts\n\n- **Prompt:** \"Update CLAUDE.md to explicitly state: We must NEVER use the LLM to parse raw CSV rows—all detection must be done with deterministic Python code. We are aiming for the Champion tier, which requires a strict validation loop on LLM output (e.g., checking title lengths in code before accepting them) and outputting fix CSV files. We must strictly follow the output contract in ../report.schema.json. Update PROMPTS.md with this request. Ensure .claude/settings.json and the audit hooks are active. Once you have updated CLAUDE.md, commit the changes with: 'chore: establish strict grading constraints and champion tier goals in project memory and CRITICAL PROCESS CHECK: Ensure that .claude/audit.jsonl is actively recording our steps via the hooks. Also, add this prompt to PROMPTS.md, and log any data parsing choices (like handling CSV encoding or empty values) in DECISIONS.md.\"\n- **For:** Establishing strict grading constraints and project goals in memory.\n- **Revised?** No.\n\n- **Prompt:** \"We are moving to the 'Ingest' and 'Detect' stages. Open seo/detector.py. Write deterministic Python code (using the pandas library) to read ../sample-export/internal_all.csv. You must programmatically detect these exact issues without using the LLM:\nMissing/Duplicate Title 1 (ignore non-HTML rows)\nTitle too long (> 561px or > 60 chars) or too short (< 30 chars)\nMissing/Duplicate Meta Description 1, or too long (> 155 chars)\nMissing H1, or Multiple/Duplicate H1s\nBroken links (Status 4xx), Server errors (5xx), Redirects (3xx)\nMissing image alt text (for image rows)\nThin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML pages)\nOrphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200 pages)\nOutput a structured dictionary of these found issues.\nPROCESS ENFORCEMENT:\nAdd an entry to DECISIONS.md explaining how the code handles CSV encoding (e.g., UTF-8 BOM), NaN values, and isolating 'text/html' vs 'image' rows.\nAppend this exact prompt to PROMPTS.md.\nRun the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv to ensure it parses successfully. If it errors (e.g., a missing column or type mismatch), debug it directly and fix it. This will show strong debugging discipline in our audit log.\nOnce it runs perfectly without crashing, run git add . and git commit -m \\\"feat: implement deterministic rulebook detection in detector.py\\\". Do not proceed until committed.\"\n- **For:** Implementing deterministic SEO rulebook detection using pandas.\n- **Revised?** No.\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f410ac72-0caa-414d-af44-ecfc16eb9dc8","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"0f620825-deea-4b38-ba0f-5cd3e3b7a6ae","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"7a6866f7-661f-4b9e-ad90-cae7241de736","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_gfbaxxdz","type":"tool_result","content":"The file seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"b764f622-b3e3-44ce-ade5-bf43c3f6f1a6","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:56:15.405Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py","oldString":"    def do_GET(self):","newString":"    def do_POST(self):\n        if self.path == \"/update\":\n            content_length = int(self.headers.get('Content-Length', 0))\n            body = self.rfile.read(content_length)\n            try:\n                data = json.loads(body)\n                with _lock:\n                    RUN.update(data)\n                _emit(\"update\", data)\n                self._send(200, json.dumps({\"status\": \"ok\"}), \"application/json\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self._send(400, json.dumps({\"error\": str(e)}), \"application/json\")\n        else:\n            self._send(404, \"not found\")\n\n    def do_GET(self):","originalFile":"\"\"\"\nserver.py — local MCP server + live dashboard host (one process, two faces).\n\n  1. MCP tools over stdio  -> Claude Code calls: seo_load, seo_detect, seo_report, seo_export\n  2. HTTP + SSE on localhost:7700 -> the live cockpit that fills as issues are found.\n\nSTARTER: works end to end out of the box. Extend the detectors (seo/detector.py) and\nthe fixes (the model-driven title rewriting / redirect map) during the Sprint.\n\nNeeds the MCP SDK to expose tools to Claude (`pip install mcp`); without it the dashboard\nstill runs so you can use run.py. Standard library otherwise.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport json, os, queue, threading, time\nfrom http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nROOT = os.path.dirname(HERE)\nDASH_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"dashboard\")\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"outputs\")\nPORT = int(os.environ.get(\"SEO_PORT\", \"7700\"))\nMODEL = os.environ.get(\"RADAR_MODEL\", \"qwen3.5:9b\")\n\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, ROOT)\nfrom seo import detector  # noqa: E402\n\nRUN = {\"site\": None, \"urls\": 0, \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None, \"status\": \"idle\"}\n_subs: list[queue.Queue] = []\n_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n\ndef _emit(event, data):\n    payload = json.dumps({\"event\": event, \"data\": data})\n    with _lock:\n        for q in list(_subs):\n            try: q.put_nowait(payload)\n            except Exception: pass\n\n\n# ----- pipeline tools (importable by run.py without MCP) -----\ndef seo_load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n    rows = detector.load_rows(export_dir)\n    RUN.update({\"rows\": rows, \"urls\": len(rows), \"issues\": [], \"summary\": None,\n                \"site\": _guess_site(rows), \"status\": \"running\"})\n    _emit(\"loaded\", {\"site\": RUN[\"site\"], \"urls\": len(rows)})\n    return {\"urls\": len(rows), \"site\": RUN[\"site\"]}\n\n\ndef _guess_site(rows):\n    if not rows: return \"unknown\"\n    addr = rows[0].get(\"Address\", \"\")\n    try:\n        from urllib.parse import urlparse\n        return urlparse(addr).netloc or \"unknown\"\n    except Exception:\n        return \"unknown\"\n\n\ndef seo_detect() -> dict:\n    issues = detector.detect(RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n    RUN[\"issues\"] = issues\n    RUN[\"summary\"] = detector.summarize(issues)\n    for i in issues:\n        _emit(\"issue\", i)\n    _emit(\"summary\", RUN[\"summary\"])\n    return {\"detected\": len(issues), \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"]}\n\n\ndef _report_obj() -> dict:\n    return {\n        \"site\": RUN[\"site\"],\n        \"urls_crawled\": RUN[\"urls\"],\n        \"summary\": RUN[\"summary\"] or {\"total_issues\": 0, \"by_severity\": {}},\n        \"issues\": RUN[\"issues\"],\n        \"fixes\": RUN.get(\"fixes\", {\"titles\": [], \"redirect_map\": []}),\n        \"recommendations\": RUN.get(\"recommendations\", []),\n        \"run_meta\": {\"model\": MODEL, \"model_calls\": RUN.get(\"model_calls\", 0),\n                     \"duration_sec\": RUN.get(\"duration_sec\", 0)},\n    }\n\n\ndef seo_set_fixes(titles=None, redirect_map=None) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"fixes\"] = {\"titles\": titles or [], \"redirect_map\": redirect_map or []}\n    _emit(\"fixes\", RUN[\"fixes\"]); return {\"titles\": len(titles or []), \"redirects\": len(redirect_map or [])}\n\n\ndef seo_recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n    RUN[\"recommendations\"] = recommendations\n    _emit(\"recommendations\", {\"recommendations\": recommendations}); return {\"count\": len(recommendations)}\n\n\ndef seo_report() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.json\")\n    json.dump(_report_obj(), open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\"), indent=2)\n    RUN[\"status\"] = \"done\"; _emit(\"saved\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef seo_export() -> dict:\n    os.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n    p = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"report.html\")\n    open(p, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\").write(_render_html(_report_obj()))\n    _emit(\"exported\", {\"path\": p}); return {\"path\": p}\n\n\ndef _render_html(o) -> str:\n    sev = (o[\"summary\"] or {}).get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    rows = \"\".join(\n        f'<tr><td><span class=\"sev {i[\"severity\"].lower()}\">{i[\"severity\"]}</span></td>'\n        f'<td>{i[\"type\"]}</td><td>{i[\"count\"]}</td>'\n        f'<td>{(i.get(\"explanation\") or \"\")}</td></tr>'\n        for i in sorted(o[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3)))\n    recs = \"\".join(f\"<li>{r}</li>\" for r in o.get(\"recommendations\", []))\n    return f\"\"\"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1\"><title>SEO Audit — {o['site']}</title>\n<style>body{{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;background:#1a1a1f;color:#f8f7f4;margin:0;padding:40px;line-height:1.5}}\n.wrap{{max-width:860px;margin:0 auto}}h1{{font-size:28px;margin:0 0 4px}}.sub{{color:#c8c5be;margin-bottom:24px}}\n.card{{background:#242428;border:1px solid #3a3a42;border-radius:14px;padding:22px;margin-bottom:18px}}\n.k{{display:flex;gap:24px;flex-wrap:wrap}}.k div{{font-size:13px;color:#c8c5be}}.k b{{display:block;font-size:30px;color:#f8f7f4}}\ntable{{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}}th,td{{text-align:left;padding:9px 10px;border-bottom:1px solid #3a3a42}}\nth{{font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#c8c5be}}\n.sev{{font-size:11px;font-weight:700;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:999px}}.sev.high{{background:#FF0000;color:#fff}}\n.sev.medium{{background:#e2b53e;color:#1a1a1f}}.sev.low{{background:#3a3a42;color:#c8c5be}}\nul{{padding-left:20px}}li{{margin:6px 0}}.muted{{color:#c8c5be;font-size:13px}}</style></head><body><div class=\"wrap\">\n<h1>SEO Audit Report</h1><div class=\"sub\">{o['site']} · {o['urls_crawled']} URLs crawled</div>\n<div class=\"card k\"><div><b>{(o['summary'] or {}).get('total_issues',0)}</b>total issues</div>\n<div><b style=\"color:#FF0000\">{sev.get('High',0)}</b>high</div><div><b style=\"color:#e2b53e\">{sev.get('Medium',0)}</b>medium</div>\n<div><b>{sev.get('Low',0)}</b>low</div></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Issues (prioritized)</h3><table><thead><tr><th>Severity</th><th>Issue</th><th>URLs</th><th>What it means</th></tr></thead>\n<tbody>{rows or '<tr><td colspan=4 class=muted>No issues detected.</td></tr>'}</tbody></table></div>\n<div class=\"card\"><h3>Recommendations</h3><ul>{recs or '<li class=muted>None generated.</li>'}</ul></div>\n<p class=\"muted\">Generated by SEO Command Center · model {o.get('run_meta',{}).get('model','')}</p></div></body></html>\"\"\"\n\n\n# ----- dashboard HTTP host -----\nclass H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n    def log_message(self, *a): pass\n    def _send(self, code, body, ctype=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"):\n        self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)\n        self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n        self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)\n    def do_GET(self):\n        if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"no dashboard\")\n        elif self.path == \"/app.js\":\n            p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"app.js\")\n            self._send(200, open(p, encoding=\"utf-8\").read() if os.path.exists(p) else \"\", \"application/javascript\")\n        elif self.path == \"/state\":\n            self._send(200, json.dumps({k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}), \"application/json\")\n        elif self.path == \"/events\":\n            self.send_response(200); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n            self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()\n            q = queue.Queue()\n            with _lock: _subs.append(q)\n            try:\n                snap = {k: v for k, v in RUN.items() if k != \"rows\"}\n                self.wfile.write(f\"data: {json.dumps({'event':'snapshot','data':snap})}\\n\\n\".encode()); self.wfile.flush()\n                while True:\n                    try: self.wfile.write(f\"data: {q.get(timeout=15)}\\n\\n\".encode())\n                    except queue.Empty: self.wfile.write(b\": ping\\n\\n\")\n                    self.wfile.flush()\n            except Exception: pass\n            finally:\n                with _lock:\n                    if q in _subs: _subs.remove(q)\n        else: self._send(404, \"not found\")\n\n\ndef start_dashboard(port=PORT):\n    httpd = ThreadingHTTPServer((\"127.0.0.1\", port), H)\n    threading.Thread(target=httpd.serve_forever, daemon=True).start()\n    return httpd\n\n\ndef _run_mcp():\n    try:\n        from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP\n    except Exception:\n        print(f\"[seo] MCP SDK not found. Dashboard only at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n        while True: time.sleep(3600)\n    mcp = FastMCP(\"seo-command-center\")\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def load(export_dir: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Load a Screaming Frog export directory (expects internal_all.csv).\"\"\"\n        return seo_load(export_dir)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def detect_issues() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Run the SEO rulebook detectors over the loaded crawl.\"\"\"\n        return seo_detect()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def set_fixes(titles: list = None, redirect_map: list = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the model-written title rewrites and the redirect map.\"\"\"\n        return seo_set_fixes(titles, redirect_map)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def recommend(recommendations: list) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Attach the prioritized recommendations.\"\"\"\n        return seo_recommend(recommendations)\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def write_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.json.\"\"\"\n        return seo_report()\n\n    @mcp.tool()\n    def export_report() -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Write outputs/report.html (the client deliverable).\"\"\"\n        return seo_export()\n\n    mcp.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    start_dashboard()\n    print(f\"[seo] dashboard live at http://localhost:{PORT}\", flush=True)\n    _run_mcp()\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":140,"oldLines":6,"newStart":140,"newLines":21,"lines":["         self.send_response(code); self.send_header(\"Content-Type\", ctype)","         self.send_header(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\"); self.end_headers()","         self.wfile.write(body.encode() if isinstance(body, str) else body)","+    def do_POST(self):","+        if self.path == \"/update\":","+            content_length = int(self.headers.get('Content-Length', 0))","+            body = self.rfile.read(content_length)","+            try:","+                data = json.loads(body)","+                with _lock:","+                    RUN.update(data)","+                _emit(\"update\", data)","+                self._send(200, json.dumps({\"status\": \"ok\"}), \"application/json\")","+            except Exception as e:","+                self._send(400, json.dumps({\"error\": str(e)}), \"application/json\")","+        else:","+            self._send(404, \"not found\")","+","     def do_GET(self):","         if self.path in (\"/\", \"/index.html\"):","             p = os.path.join(DASH_DIR, \"index.html\")"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0f620825-deea-4b38-ba0f-5cd3e3b7a6ae","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}
{"parentUuid":"b764f622-b3e3-44ce-ade5-bf43c3f6f1a6","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_8ec793749e223d2d28b4a9b6","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_7slby4eh","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py","content":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\nCoded with pandas for efficiency and robust NaN handling.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport pandas as pd\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom urllib import request, parse\n\ndef send_update(data: dict):\n    \"\"\"Send status updates to the MCP server dashboard.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        url = \"http://localhost:7700/update\"\n        req = request.Request(url, data=json.dumps(data).encode(),\n                             headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})\n        with request.urlopen(req, timeout=0.5) as resp:\n            pass\n    except Exception:\n        # Silent fail to avoid interrupting detection if server is down\n        pass\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    # utf-8-sig handles UTF-8 with BOM\n    df = pd.read_csv(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\")\n    # Replace NaN with empty strings for consistent processing\n    df = df.fillna('')\n    return df\n\ndef detect(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Deterministic SEO issue detection based on the rulebook.\n    Returns a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    \"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add_issue(issue_type, severity, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(list(set(urls)))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\n                \"type\": issue_type,\n                \"severity\": severity,\n                \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                \"count\": len(urls),\n                \"explanation\": explanation\n            })\n\n    # --- Pre-filtering ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Filtering rows\", \"urls_processed\": 0})\n\n    # HTML rows (for most checks)\n    df_html = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('text/html', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # Indexable 200 pages (for title/meta/orphan checks)\n    df_idx200 = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable')\n    ]\n\n    # Image rows (for alt text check)\n    df_images = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('image/', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Detecting issues\", \"urls_processed\": len(df)})\n\n    # --- 1. Titles ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Titles\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    # Missing Title (Indexable 200)\n    missing_title_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_title\", \"High\", missing_title_urls, \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Titles (Indexable)\n    df_idx = df_html[df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable']\n    titles = df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip()\n    title_counts = titles.value_counts()\n    dup_titles = title_counts[title_counts > 1].index\n    all_dup_title_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_titles) & (df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", all_dup_title_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    # Title too long (> 561px or > 60 chars)\n    too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Pixel Width'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 561) |\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 60)\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\", too_long_urls, \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # Title too short (< 30 chars and not empty)\n    too_short_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 30) &\n        (df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\", too_short_urls, \"Titles that are too short to be descriptive.\")\n\n    # --- 2. Meta Descriptions ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Metas\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    # Missing Meta Description (Indexable 200)\n    missing_meta_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\", missing_meta_urls, \"Indexable pages with no meta description.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Meta Descriptions (Indexable)\n    meta_descriptions = df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip()\n    meta_counts = meta_descriptions.value_counts()\n    dup_meta_vals = meta_counts[meta_counts > 1].index\n    dup_meta_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_meta_vals) & (df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_meta_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    # Meta too long (> 155 chars)\n    meta_too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Meta Description 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 155\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\", meta_too_long_urls, \"Meta descriptions likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- 3. H1s ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking H1s\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    # Missing H1 (200 page)\n    missing_h1_urls = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['H1-1'].str.strip() == '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\", missing_h1_urls, \"Pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate H1s (Indexable)\n    h1s = df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip()\n    h1_counts = h1s.value_counts()\n    dup_h1_vals = h1_counts[h1_counts > 1].index\n    dup_h1_urls = df_idx[df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip().isin(dup_h1_vals) & (df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Multiple H1s\n    if 'H1-2' in df.columns:\n        multiple_h1_urls = df_html[df_html['H1-2'].str.strip() != '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"multiple_h1\", \"Low\", multiple_h1_urls, \"Pages with more than one H1 tag.\")\n\n    # --- 4. Status Codes ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Status Codes\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    status_codes = pd.to_numeric(df['Status Code'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)\n\n    # Broken links (4xx)\n    broken_urls = df[(status_codes >= 400) & (status_codes <= 499)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"broken_link\", \"High\", broken_urls, \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n\n    # Server errors (5xx)\n    server_error_urls = df[(status_codes >= 500) & (status_codes <= 599)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"server_error\", \"High\", server_error_urls, \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n\n    # Redirects (3xx)\n    redirect_urls = df[(status_codes >= 300) & (status_codes <= 399)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"redirect\", \"Medium\", redirect_urls, \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- 5. Image Alt Text ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Images\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    alt_col = 'Alt Text' if 'Alt Text' in df.columns else 'Alt Text 1'\n    if alt_col in df.columns:\n        missing_alt_urls = df_images[df_images[alt_col].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"missing_image_alt_text\", \"Low\", missing_alt_urls, \"Images missing alt text.\")\n\n    # --- 6. Content & Links ---\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Content\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)\n    thin_content_urls = df_idx[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"thin_content\", \"Low\", thin_content_urls, \"Pages with very low word count.\")\n\n    # Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200)\n    orphan_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Inlinks'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) == 0\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")\n\n    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Complete\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})\n    return issues\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"]\n    return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),\n            \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]\n    try:\n        df = load_rows(export_path)\n        iss = detect(df)\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n        print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n        for i in iss:\n            print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error during execution: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":49813,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":5620,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"717a744a-26c7-4df4-8dcb-ded9acb01feb","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:56:53.076Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

H1s ---","+    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking H1s\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})","     # Missing H1 (200 page)","     missing_h1_urls = df_html[","         (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &"]},{"oldStart":115,"oldLines":13,"newStart":129,"newLines":13,"lines":["     dup_h1_urls = df_idx[df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip().isin(dup_h1_vals) & (df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()","     add_issue(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")"," ","-    # Multiple H1s (Prompt asked for \"Multiple/Duplicate H1s\")","-    # Note: Screaming Frog usually only reports H1-1. Status Codes ---","+    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Status Codes\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})","     status_codes = pd.to_numeric(df['Status Code'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)"," ","     # Broken links (4xx)"]},{"oldStart":137,"oldLines":17,"newStart":151,"newLines":14,"lines":["     add_issue(\"redirect\", \"Medium\", redirect_urls, \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")"," ","     # --- 5. Image Alt Text ---","-    # Missing alt text (for image rows)","-    # Usually the column is 'Alt Text'","+    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Images\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})","     alt_col = 'Alt Text' if 'Alt Text' in df.columns else 'Alt Text 1'","     if alt_col in df.columns:","         missing_alt_urls = df_images[df_images[alt_col].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()","         add_issue(\"missing_image_alt_text\", \"Low\", missing_alt_urls, \"Images missing alt text.\")","-    else:","-        # Log this if we can't find the column","-        pass"," ","     # --- 6. Content & Links ---","+    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Checking Content\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})","     # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)","     thin_content_urls = df_idx[","         pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200"]},{"oldStart":160,"oldLines":24,"newStart":171,"newLines":19,"lines":["     ]['Address'].tolist()","     add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")"," ","+    send_update({\"current_stage\": \"Complete\", \"issues_found\": len(issues)})","     return issues"," "," def summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:","     by_sev = defaultdict(int)","     for i in issues:","-        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"] # Total affected URLs, not issue types","+        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"]","     return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),","             \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),","             \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}"," "," if __name__ == \"__main__\":","-    # Using the relative path provided in the prompt","     export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]","-","-    # Adjusted path for when running from inside seo-command-center/","-    # The prompt says \"run the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv\"","-    # If we run from the root, we might need to be careful.","-","     try:","         df = load_rows(export_path)","         iss = detect(df)"]}],"originalFile":"\"\"\"\ndetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\nCoded with pandas for efficiency and robust NaN handling.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport pandas as pd\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n    # utf-8-sig handles UTF-8 with BOM\n    df = pd.read_csv(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\")\n    # Replace NaN with empty strings for consistent processing\n    df = df.fillna('')\n    return df\n\ndef detect(df: pd.DataFrame) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Deterministic SEO issue detection based on the rulebook.\n    Returns a list of issue dicts: {type, severity, affected_urls, count, explanation}.\n    \"\"\"\n    issues = []\n\n    def add_issue(issue_type, severity, urls, explanation):\n        urls = sorted(list(set(urls)))\n        if urls:\n            issues.append({\n                \"type\": issue_type,\n                \"severity\": severity,\n                \"affected_urls\": urls,\n                \"count\": len(urls),\n                \"explanation\": explanation\n            })\n\n    # --- Pre-filtering ---\n    # HTML rows (for most checks)\n    df_html = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('text/html', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # Indexable 200 pages (for title/meta/orphan checks)\n    df_idx200 = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable')\n    ]\n\n    # Image rows (for alt text check)\n    df_images = df[df['Content Type'].str.contains('image/', case=False, na=False)]\n\n    # --- 1. Titles ---\n    # Missing Title (Indexable 200)\n    missing_title_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_title\", \"High\", missing_title_urls, \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Titles (Indexable)\n    # We check indexable pages generally for duplicates, as per rulebook\n    df_idx = df_html[df_html['Indexability'].str.lower() == 'indexable']\n    titles = df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip()\n    duplicates = titles[titles.duplicated()].index\n    dup_title_urls = df_idx.loc[duplicates, 'Address'].tolist()\n    # Note: This identifies any page that has a title already seen.\n    # To get all pages sharing a title (including the first one):\n    title_counts = titles.value_counts()\n    dup_titles = title_counts[title_counts > 1].index\n    all_dup_title_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_titles) & (df_idx['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\", all_dup_title_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n\n    # Title too long (> 561px or > 60 chars)\n    # Using .astype(float).fillna(0) to handle numeric conversion safely\n    too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Pixel Width'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 561) |\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 60)\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\", too_long_urls, \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # Title too short (< 30 chars and not empty)\n    too_short_urls = df_idx200[\n        (pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Title 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 30) &\n        (df_idx200['Title 1'].str.strip() != '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\", too_short_urls, \"Titles that are too short to be descriptive.\")\n\n    # --- 2. Meta Descriptions ---\n    # Missing Meta Description (Indexable 200)\n    missing_meta_urls = df_idx200[df_idx200['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\", missing_meta_urls, \"Indexable pages with no meta description.\")\n\n    # Duplicate Meta Descriptions (Indexable)\n    meta_descriptions = df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip()\n    meta_counts = meta_descriptions.value_counts()\n    dup_meta_vals = meta_counts[meta_counts > 1].index\n    dup_meta_urls = df_idx[df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip().isin(dup_meta_vals) & (df_idx['Meta Description 1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\", dup_meta_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n\n    # Meta too long (> 155 chars)\n    meta_too_long_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Meta Description 1 Length'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 155\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\", meta_too_long_urls, \"Meta descriptions likely truncated in search results.\")\n\n    # --- 3. H1s ---\n    # Missing H1 (200 page)\n    missing_h1_urls = df_html[\n        (df_html['Status Code'].astype(str) == '200') &\n        (df_html['H1-1'].str.strip() == '')\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\", missing_h1_urls, \"Pages missing an H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Duplicate H1s (Indexable)\n    h1s = df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip()\n    h1_counts = h1s.value_counts()\n    dup_h1_vals = h1_counts[h1_counts > 1].index\n    dup_h1_urls = df_idx[df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip().isin(dup_h1_vals) & (df_idx['H1-1'].str.strip() != '')]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\", dup_h1_urls, \"Pages sharing an identical H1 tag.\")\n\n    # Multiple H1s (Prompt asked for \"Multiple/Duplicate H1s\")\n    # Note: Screaming Frog usually only reports H1-1. If there's an H1-2 column, we check it.\n    if 'H1-2' in df.columns:\n        multiple_h1_urls = df_html[df_html['H1-2'].str.strip() != '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"multiple_h1\", \"Low\", multiple_h1_urls, \"Pages with more than one H1 tag.\")\n\n    # --- 4. Status Codes ---\n    status_codes = pd.to_numeric(df['Status Code'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)\n\n    # Broken links (4xx)\n    broken_urls = df[(status_codes >= 400) & (status_codes <= 499)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"broken_link\", \"High\", broken_urls, \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n\n    # Server errors (5xx)\n    server_error_urls = df[(status_codes >= 500) & (status_codes <= 599)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"server_error\", \"High\", server_error_urls, \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n\n    # Redirects (3xx)\n    redirect_urls = df[(status_codes >= 300) & (status_codes <= 399)]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"redirect\", \"Medium\", redirect_urls, \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n\n    # --- 5. Image Alt Text ---\n    # Missing alt text (for image rows)\n    # Usually the column is 'Alt Text'\n    alt_col = 'Alt Text' if 'Alt Text' in df.columns else 'Alt Text 1'\n    if alt_col in df.columns:\n        missing_alt_urls = df_images[df_images[alt_col].str.strip() == '']['Address'].tolist()\n        add_issue(\"missing_image_alt_text\", \"Low\", missing_alt_urls, \"Images missing alt text.\")\n    else:\n        # Log this if we can't find the column\n        pass\n\n    # --- 6. Content & Links ---\n    # Thin content (Word Count < 200 on indexable HTML)\n    thin_content_urls = df_idx[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx['Word Count'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) < 200\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"thin_content\", \"Low\", thin_content_urls, \"Pages with very low word count.\")\n\n    # Orphan pages (Inlinks = 0 on indexable 200)\n    orphan_urls = df_idx200[\n        pd.to_numeric(df_idx200['Inlinks'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) == 0\n    ]['Address'].tolist()\n    add_issue(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\", orphan_urls, \"Indexable pages with zero internal links.\")\n\n    return issues\n\ndef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n    for i in issues:\n        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += i[\"count\"] # Total affected URLs, not issue types\n    return {\"total_issue_types\": len(issues),\n            \"total_affected_urls\": sum(i[\"count\"] for i in issues),\n            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Using the relative path provided in the prompt\n    export_path = \"../sample-export\" if len(sys.argv) < 2 else sys.argv[1]\n\n    # Adjusted path for when running from inside seo-command-center/\n    # The prompt says \"run the script against ../sample-export/internal_all.csv\"\n    # If we run from the root, we might need to be careful.\n\n    try:\n        df = load_rows(export_path)\n        iss = detect(df)\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n        print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n        for i in iss:\n            print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<24} x{i['count']}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error during execution: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"717a744a-26c7-4df4-8dcb-ded9acb01feb","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"57914421-29ba-44c2-be2f-ce1e3a71f273","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_244457f40973005fa4d1ce4a","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I have implemented the backend state management system for the MCP dashboard.\n\n### Summary of Changes:\n\n1.  **`seo-command-center\\mcp\\server.py`**:\n    *   Added a `do_POST` method to the `H` request handler.\n    *   Implemented a `/update` endpoint that accepts JSON payloads to update the global `RUN` state and triggers an SSE `update` event for any connected dashboard clients.\n2.  **`seo-command-center\\seo\\detector.py`**:\n    *   Implemented a `send_update` helper function using `urllib.request` to send asynchronous status updates to the server.\n    *   Integrated if (l.querySelector(\".empty\")) l.innerHTML = \"\";\n7\t  const d = document.createElement(\"div\"); d.textContent = \"› \" + msg; l.appendChild(d); l.scrollTop = l.scrollHeight;\n8\t}\n9\tfunction addIssue(i) {\n10\t  const tb = $(\"tbody\"); if (tb.querySelector(\".empty\")) tb.innerHTML = \"\";\n11\t  const tr = document.createElement(\"tr\");\n12\t  tr.innerHTML = `<td><span class=\"sev ${i.severity.toLowerCase()}\">${i.severity}</span></td>\n13\t                  <td>${i.type}</td><td>${i.count}</td>`;\n14\t  tb.appendChild(tr);\n15\t  totals[i.severity] = (totals[i.severity] || 0) + 1; totals.total++;\n16\t  $(\"c-total\").textContent = totals.total; $(\"c-high\").textContent = totals.High;\n17\t  $(\"c-med\").textContent = totals.Medium; $(\"c-low\").textContent = totals.Low;\n18\t}\n19\tfunction handle({ event, data }) {\n20\t  if (event === \"snapshot\") {\n21\t    if (data.site) { $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = (data.urls||0) + \" URLs\"; }\n22\t    (data.issues || []).forEach(addIssue);\n23\t  } else if (event === \"loaded\") {\n24\t    $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = data.urls + \" URLs\";\n25\t    log(`Loaded ${data.urls} URLs from ${data.site}`); $(\"tbody\").innerHTML = \"\";\n26\t    totals = { High:0, Medium:0, Low:0, total:0 };\n27\t  } else if (event === \"issue\") { addIssue(data); log(`Found ${data.count} × ${data.type}`); }\n28\t  else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }\n29\t  else if (event === \"fixes\") { log(`Fixes ready: ${(data.titles||[]).length} titles, ${(data.redirect_map||[]).length} redirects`); }\n30\t  else if (event === \"exported\") { $(\"export\").innerHTML = \"<b>report.html written ✓</b><br><span style='color:#c8c5be;font-size:12px'>Open or email outputs/report.html to the client.</span>\"; }\n31\t  else if (event === \"saved\") { log(\"report.json saved\"); }\n32\t}\n33\tconst es = new EventSource(\"/events\");\n34\tes.onmessage = (m) => { try { handle(JSON.parse(m.data)); } catch (e) {} };\n35\t"}]},"uuid":"7d8a27f7-78d8-411e-b7e1-f1ce8725c810","timestamp":"2026-06-06T10:00:23.654Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\dashboard\\app.js","content":"/* app.js — SEO Command Center live cockpit. Plain DOM + SSE, no build step. */\nconst $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);\nlet totals = { High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0, total: 0 };\n\nfunction log(msg) {\n  const l = $(\"log\"); if (l.querySelector(\".empty\")) l.innerHTML = \"\";\n  const d = document.createElement(\"div\"); d.textContent = \"› \" + msg; l.appendChild(d); l.scrollTop = l.scrollHeight;\n}\nfunction addIssue(i) {\n  const tb = $(\"tbody\"); if (tb.querySelector(\".empty\")) tb.innerHTML = \"\";\n  const tr = document.createElement(\"tr\");\n  tr.innerHTML = `<td><span class=\"sev ${i.severity.toLowerCase()}\">${i.severity}</span></td>\n                  <td>${i.type}</td><td>${i.count}</td>`;\n  tb.appendChild(tr);\n  totals[i.severity] = (totals[i.severity] || 0) + 1; totals.total++;\n  $(\"c-total\").textContent = totals.total; $(\"c-high\").textContent = totals.High;\n  $(\"c-med\").textContent = totals.Medium; $(\"c-low\").textContent = totals.Low;\n}\nfunction handle({ event, data }) {\n  if (event === \"snapshot\") {\n    if (data.site) { $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = (data.urls||0) + \" URLs\"; }\n    (data.issues || []).forEach(addIssue);\n  } else if (event === \"loaded\") {\n    $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = data.urls + \" URLs\";\n    log(`Loaded ${data.urls} URLs from ${data.site}`); $(\"tbody\").innerHTML = \"\";\n    totals = { High:0, Medium:0, Low:0, total:0 };\n  } else if (event === \"issue\") { addIssue(data); log(`Found ${data.count} × ${data.type}`); }\n  else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }\n  else if (event === \"fixes\") { log(`Fixes ready: ${(data.titles||[]).length} titles, ${(data.redirect_map||[]).length} redirects`); }\n  else if (event === \"exported\") { $(\"export\").innerHTML = \"<b>report.html written ✓</b><br><span style='color:#c8c5be;font-size:12px'>Open or email outputs/report.html to the client.</span>\"; }\n  else if (event === \"saved\") { log(\"report.json saved\"); }\n}\nconst es = new EventSource(\"/events\");\nes.onmessage = (m) => { try { handle(JSON.parse(m.data)); } catch (e) {} };\n","numLines":35,"startLine":1,"totalLines":35}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e7431730-2945-4c83-bbae-8b4362b3d564","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}

{"parentUuid":"80c17c3c-449a-47dc-becb-652fc30dc1a6","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_3872f6d747ff16ad7270322b","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"gemma4:31b","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants to implement a real-time frontend for the SEO Command Center dashboard using SSE.\n\nLooking at the provided files:\n- `dashboard/index.html`: Already has a structure with status indicators, counts, a table for issues, and a run log.\n- `dashboard/app.js`: Already has an `EventSource` listening to `/events` and a `handle` function to process different events (`snapshot`, `loaded`, `issue`, `summary`, etc.).\n\nWait, the provided `app.js` actually seems to *already* implement most of what is requested.\n- `const es = new EventSource(\"/events\");`\n- `es.onmessage = (m) => { try { handle(JSON.parse(m.data)); } catch (e) {} };`\n- `handle` processes `event === \"issue\"`, `event === \"loaded\"`, etc.\n\nHowever, the user's prompt says \"Update dashboard/index.html and dashboard/app.js to connect to our MCP server... implement an EventSource... The UI must look clean, product-grade... clearly display the current pipeline stage... along with a live, updating count\".\n\nThe existing `app.js` handles `issue`, `loaded`, `summary`, etc., but it doesn't seem to handle the `update` event I added to `server.py` in the previous turn:\n```python\n# In server.py\n_emit(\"update\", data)\n```\nAnd the prompt specifically mentioned: \"receive live updates from detector.py (like 'urls_processed', 'current_stage', 'issues_found')\".\n\nSo I need to:\n1. Update `app.js` to handle the `update` event and update the UI (pipeline stage, counts).\n2. Improve the UI in `index.html` if necessary to better represent the \"current stage\".\n3. Ensure `server.py` handles CORS if the dashboard is accessed via a different port/domain (though it's usually served from the same server, it's good to check). Update `app.js` to handle `event === \"update\"`.\n2. Update `index.html` slightly if needed for better stage visibility.\n3. Log the SSE architecture in `DECISIONS.md`.\n4. Append the prompt to `PROMPTS.md`.\n5. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"654ee3df-8a08-495c-b373-5462b6a520bb","timestamp":"2026-06-06T10:01:22.478Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"seo-command-center\\dashboard\\app.js","oldString":"  else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }","newString":"  else if (event === \"update\") {\n    if (data.current_stage) { $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.current_stage; }\n    if (data.urls_processed !== undefined) { $(\"urls\").textContent = data.urls_processed + \" URLs\"; }\n    if (data.issues_found !== undefined) {\n      $(\"c-total\").textContent = data.issues_found;\n      totals.total = data.issues_found;\n    }\n    log(`Pipeline: ${data.current_stage || 'Processing...'}`);\n  }\n  else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }","originalFile":"/* app.js — SEO Command Center live cockpit. Plain DOM + SSE, no build step. */\nconst $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);\nlet totals = { High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0, total: 0 };\n\nfunction log(msg) {\n  const l = $(\"log\"); if (l.querySelector(\".empty\")) l.innerHTML = \"\";\n  const d = document.createElement(\"div\"); d.textContent = \"› \" + msg; l.appendChild(d); l.scrollTop = l.scrollHeight;\n}\nfunction addIssue(i) {\n  const tb = $(\"tbody\"); if (tb.querySelector(\".empty\")) tb.innerHTML = \"\";\n  const tr = document.createElement(\"tr\");\n  tr.innerHTML = `<td><span class=\"sev ${i.severity.toLowerCase()}\">${i.severity}</span></td>\n                  <td>${i.type}</td><td>${i.count}</td>`;\n  tb.appendChild(tr);\n  totals[i.severity] = (totals[i.severity] || 0) + 1; totals.total++;\n  $(\"c-total\").textContent = totals.total; $(\"c-high\").textContent = totals.High;\n  $(\"c-med\").textContent = totals.Medium; $(\"c-low\").textContent = totals.Low;\n}\nfunction handle({ event, data }) {\n  if (event === \"snapshot\") {\n    if (data.site) { $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = (data.urls||0) + \" URLs\"; }\n    (data.issues || []).forEach(addIssue);\n  } else if (event === \"loaded\") {\n    $(\"meta\").textContent = \"· \" + data.site; $(\"urls\").textContent = data.urls + \" URLs\";\n    log(`Loaded ${data.urls} URLs from ${data.site}`); $(\"tbody\").innerHTML = \"\";\n    totals = { High:0, Medium:0, Low:0, total:0 };\n  } else if (event === \"issue\") { addIssue(data); log(`Found ${data.count} × ${data.type}`); }\n  else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }\n  else if (event === \"fixes\") { log(`Fixes ready: ${(data.titles||[]).length} titles, ${(data.redirect_map||[]).length} redirects`); }\n  else if (event === \"exported\") { $(\"export\").innerHTML = \"<b>report.html written ✓</b><br><span style='color:#c8c5be;font-size:12px'>Open or email outputs/report.html to the client.</span>\"; }\n  else if (event === \"saved\") { log(\"report.json saved\"); }\n}\nconst es = new EventSource(\"/events\");\nes.onmessage = (m) => { try { handle(JSON.parse(m.data)); } catch (e) {} };\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":25,"oldLines":6,"newStart":25,"newLines":15,"lines":["     log(`Loaded ${data.urls} URLs from ${data.site}`); }","+    if (data.urls_processed !== undefined) { $(\"urls\").textContent = data.urls_processed + \" URLs\"; }","+    if (data.issues_found !== undefined) {","+      $(\"c-total\").textContent = data.issues_found;","+      totals.total = data.issues_found;","+    }","+    log(`Pipeline: ${data.current_stage || 'Processing...'}`);","+  }","   else if (event === \"summary\") { log(`Audit complete: ${data.total_issues} issue types`); }","   else if (event === \"fixes\") { log(`Fixes ready: ${(data.titles||[]).length} titles, ${(data.redirect_map||[]).length} redirects`); }","   else if (event === \"exported\") { $(\"export\").innerHTML = \"<b>report.html written ✓</b><br><span style='color:#c8c5be;font-size:12px'>Open or email outputs/report.html to the client.</span>\"; }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"db999fef-5302-4296-bb1d-77092d5b85c0","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"C:\\Users\\Dell\\Desktop\\nmg6-6-26\\forge3","sessionId":"4f71a01e-0a5f-457f-a436-6a810efd815d","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"prancy-questing-willow"}
